parsed_questions.question_text: What is the pKa of a sp hybridized carbon atom? A) 25 B) 35 C) 45 D) 50

A) choose the letter of the molecule with the lowest barrier to rotation (or ring-flip). B) select the letter of the molecule with the highest barrier to rotation (or ring-flip). A) ethane B) propane C) butane D) cyclohexane
Rank the following substituents in order of priority (1 = highest priority): -CH=CH2 -CN -CH2NH2 -CH2Br

If a chiral molecule has an absolute configuration of R, which direction does it rotate the plane of polarized light? A) clockwise (dextrorotary) B) counterclockwise (levarotatory) C) it doesn’t rotate the plane of polarized light D) can’t be determined from the information given

A) Enter the pKa value for each acid in the boxes below. B) Indicate whether the reactants or products will be favored at equilibrium by choosing the appropriate set of equilibrium arrows (a longer arrow is drawn toward the species favored at equilibrium). The equilibria shown are: 1) phenol + HO⁻ ⇌ phenolate ion + H₂O, with boxes for pKa values below phenol and water. 2) acetate ion + protonated methylamine ⇌ acetic acid + methylamine, with boxes for pKa values below acetate ion and acetic acid.

A) Provide structures in the boxes to complete the following acid-base (proton-transfer) reactions. B) For each set of reactants, draw in all lone pairs of electrons and show the electron movement by using curved arrows. C) Indicate whether the reactants or products will be favored at equilibrium by choosing the appropriate set of equilibrium arrows. Two reaction pairs are shown: 1) methylamine + protonated methylamine ⇌ two species structures to be completed in boxes. 2) trifluoroacetate ion + proton ⇌ trifluoroacetic acid + acetic acid, with boxes for missing structures to complete the reaction shown.
parsed_questions.canonical_answer: A) box_1=CH3-NH2; box_2=CH3-NH3+; box_3=CH3-NH3+; box_4=CH3-NH2; box_5=CF3COO-; box_6=CF3COOH; box_7=CH3COOH

parsed_questions.question_text:
Rank the following sets of molecules in order of acidity (1 = most acidic).

First set: Molecules with protonated atoms (N, S, P, Cl, C) bonded to CH3 groups:
- Protonated N with two CH3 groups
- Protonated S with one CH3 group
- Protonated P with two CH3 groups
- Protonated Cl
- Protonated C with three CH3 groups

Second set: Protonated molecules bearing O, S, or N attached to a carbonyl or similar group, showing positive charge on oxygen:
- CH3C(=O)SH protonated at O
- CH3C(=S)CH3 protonated at S
- CH3C(=O)NH2 protonated at O
- CH3C(=O)CH3 protonated at O
- CH3C(=O)OH protonated at O

Met-enkephalin, an endorphin, serves as a natural pain reliever that changes or removes the perception of nerve signals. Label all of the functional groups present in Met-enkephalin. The structure shows multiple functional groups circled at different positions. Identify functional groups such as amine, amide, phenol, arene, carboxylic acid, and sulfide in the structure.
parsed_questions.canonical_answer: amine; phenol; amide; arene; carboxylic acid; sulfide

parsed_questions.question_text: Name the following molecules: 1) A branched alkane with substituents at positions 3, 4, 5, and 6 labeled with methyl and isopropyl groups. 2) A cycloheptane ring substituted with bromine at position 1 and a tert-butyl group at position 4. Provide the IUPAC names for both molecules.

A) Draw the 4 major resonance contributors for the molecule shown in the first box. Partially completed structures are provided as a time-saver. Do not generate any additional charges. B) Draw in all lone pairs of electrons and use arrows to show the movement of electrons within the structure. C) Place a checkmark in the small boxes of the three structures that contribute the most to the resonance hybrid. D) Choose all the nucleophilic atoms in the structure at the bottom of the page.
parsed_questions.canonical_answer: The three resonance contributors with checkmarks are the two structures in the top-left, top-right and bottom-right boxes; nucleophilic atoms circled in the bottom-right molecular structure are the oxygen atoms (two keto oxygens) and the nitrogen atom (N-CH3 group).

parsed_questions.question_text:
A) Circle the structures that represent a conformation of 2,2-dimethylbutane sighting along any C-C bond. (Five Newman projections are given, including a 2,2-dimethylbutane molecular structure with carbons numbered 1 to 4, and Newman projections showing various substituents: Me, tBu, Et, H).

B) Complete each of the Newman projections below to show the most stable and least stable conformations of 2,2-dimethylbutane, sighting along the C2-C3 bond. (Two incomplete Newman projections are shown with labels at carbon 2 positions.)

C) Use the above Newman projections to calculate the barrier to rotation of 2,2-methylbutane sighting along the C2-C3 bond.
parsed_questions.canonical_answer: A) Second, third, and fourth Newman projections circled; B) Completed Newman projections with correct substituents (not explicitly described because answer is graphical); C) 3.6 kcal/mol

parsed_questions.question_text: A) Draw in the substituents on the ring flipped conformers of each molecule (A and B) to predict which molecule is lower in energy. B) Show the Newman projection for each ring-flipped conformer, sighting along the C5-C6 and C3-C2 bonds. C) Select the chair conformation that is lower in energy for each molecule. D) Which structure is lower in energy (select one)? A or B E) Briefly explain your choice.
parsed_questions.canonical_answer: D) A; E) A has fewer 1,3-diaxial interactions in its lowest energy conformation

parsed_questions.question_text: A) Label each molecule as chiral (C), achiral (A), or achiral/meso (M). B) Designate each chirality center as R or S. Three molecules are shown: 1) bicyclic structure with CH3, 2) molecule with NH2, Br, OH, N—C region with wedges and dashes, 3) a cyclohexane-like structure with CH3 and OH groups arranged on a 3D projection.

Indicate the relationships between the two molecules as enantiomers (E), diastereomers (D), or same molecule (S). Three pairs of molecules are given: 1) Two cyclopentane molecules each with two OH substituents, one wedge and one dash; 2) two cyclohexane-like structures with Br, CH3, OH, CH2OH substituents on wedges/dashes; 3) two open-chain molecules with Br, OH, CH3 substituents depicted with wedges/dashes.

(4 pts) One of the steps in fat biosynthesis is the hydration of crotonate to yield 3-hydroxybutyrate. The reaction occurs by addition of -OH to the si face at C3, followed by protonation at C2, also from the si face. A) Label the top face of each alkene carbon atom as re or si. B) Show the stereochemistry of the product and C) label C3 as R or S. The reaction scheme shows crotonate (H3C-CH=CH-CO2-, with C3 labeled as re and C2 labeled as si) converting to 3-hydroxybutyrate (H3C-CH(OH)-CH(H)-CO2-).
parsed_questions.canonical_answer: A) Top face labels: C3 = re, C2 = si; B) Product stereochemistry: OH on C3 is up (wedge) with S configuration; C) C3 is labeled S
